title: serpyllum vulgare
intro: Serpyllum vulgare, also known as wild thyme or creeping thyme, is a low-growing, flowering perennial plant native to Europe and the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The health benefits of Serpyllum vulgare include its ability to boost the immune system, improve digestion, and reduce stress and anxiety. Serpyllum vulgare can be consumed as a tea, used as a tincture, or added to food as a flavoring agent, allowing for various herbal preparations to be made from this versatile plant.
